initial_draft:
<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> ZUP: 100 </my resources>
<my goals> Buy resources with <ZUP>. You are willing to pay at most X: 60 ZUP for the resources. </my goals>
<reason> RED offered X for 60 ZUP, which is my maximum willingness to pay. Therefore, I will accept this trade. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Sounds good, RED! I accept your offer of X for 60 ZUP. </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: Looks OK. All tags are present and in the correct order. The content within each tag seems valid.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting at 60 ZUP is exactly at my maximum willingness to pay, so it maximizes my utility.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. RED offered 60 ZUP, and I accepted. This is a straightforward acceptance, so it should not be rejected.
4. Consistency: OK. The <reason> clearly states why I am accepting the trade, and the <message> reinforces that acceptance.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I used only one proposal and did not exceed my proposal limit.
</critique>
